title: Alpha runway setup and review-drop hygiene
summary: Repair the handoff surface, review archive shape, and alpha-runway operating model before a long campaign starts.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane-1: Create the clean review-drop checklist, add/refresh package-ignore guidance, and prove build/test/validate from a source-complete archive.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: A clean source review drop exists without node_modules; queue/packets validate on a complete repo; the four lane chains are ready without cross-lane collisions.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Create the clean review-drop checklist, add/refresh package-ignore guidance, and prove build/test/validate from a source-complete archive.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Sync README/progress/project-memory language to the completed High Pass alpha arc without inflating product promises.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_327_target: Make current public/project language acknowledge the completed alpha arc through filed High Pass while keeping the alpha runway framed as hardening and feedback readiness.
recommended_files: .agents/project-memory.md if a current summary sentence needs the same completed-arc wording

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Build a screenshot-proof manifest for the current alpha arc so spatial regressions have named comparison frames.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Draft the full alpha playthrough checklist from first beach outing through filed High Pass and identify route-state proof gaps.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_329_target: Create one reviewer-facing alpha route playthrough checklist from fresh `Shore Shelter` through filed `High Pass`, with route-state proof gaps separated from later instrumentation and smoke-matrix packets.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

main_326_focus.target: Create a reproducible lane-1 review-drop path that proves a source-complete archive can install, validate agents, run tests, and build without `node_modules` or generated browser-output folders.
verification_targets: `npm run review:pack` creates a timestamped archive under ignored output
`npm run review:verify -- <archive>` extracts into an ignored temp folder, runs `npm ci`, then runs `npm run validate:agents`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`
the archive contains `package-lock.json` and all source paths needed by agent validation
the archive does not contain `node_modules`, `dist`, `output`, `test-results`, `.git`, or local generated bulk
non_goals: Do not change runtime gameplay, station state, route behavior, or content copy.
Do not package external dependency folders.
Do not use `npm pack` as the final proof path unless it is made to include `package-lock.json` and all validation paths.

main_328_focus.target: Create a source-tracked alpha screenshot-proof manifest so lane-3 spatial review has stable named comparison frames without committing generated browser screenshots.
verification_targets: `npm run validate:agents` passes after queue and packet updates
the manifest names stable frame ids, purpose, existing proof path, fresh capture path, state fields, and pass/fail criteria for each representative spatial frame
every referenced existing proof path in the manifest is checked against the local workspace
generated screenshots remain under ignored `output/` paths instead of being committed
non_goals: Do not change biome geometry, traversal behavior, route state, station chrome, save schema, or science copy.
Do not create a broad visual QA framework or all-surface screenshot matrix.
Do not commit generated screenshots, browser output, or state dumps.

main_329_focus.target: Create the lane-4 alpha route playthrough checklist that lets reviewers follow the current arc from fresh `Shore Shelter` through filed `High Pass` while seeing which route-state proofs already exist and which are deferred to later preflight packets.
checklist_must_cover: `forest-hidden-hollow`, `forest-moisture-holders`, and `forest-survey-slice` through the first station return
`treeline-high-pass` active, ready-to-file, filed notice, and post-filed cleared route state
route_state_columns: required route slots or completion condition
active-window or replay label if any
known_proof_gaps_to_record: the full fresh-save-to-filed-High-Pass route path is not documented in one human-readable checklist yet
existing route-state proof is scattered across `field-requests.test.ts`, `field-request-controller.test.ts`, `field-season-board.test.ts`, `guided-field-season.test.ts`, and `runtime-smoke.test.ts`
packet `131` should own debug instrumentation/save snapshots rather than this documentation pass
packet `133` should own the deterministic route-state progression matrix rather than this documentation pass
verification_targets: `npm run validate:agents` passes after packet and queue updates
the new checklist is docs-only and does not edit route runtime, station UI, broad science copy, geometry, or support behavior
non_goals: Do not add or change Route v2 behavior.
Do not add new station pages, support UI, route frameworks, or tests in packet `130` lane 4.
Do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, or a broader planner shell.
